stdout:
## Acceptance Criteria

Pass. Repo-wide validation is implemented through `label-uniqueness` with `scope = "all"` in [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:677), evaluated in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:403), and covered by the cross-epic example at [docs/examples/cross-epic/rules.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/examples/cross-epic/rules.toml:26).

The prior missing performance criterion is now addressed: the 400-issue fixture measures elapsed time around graph evaluation and asserts a bound in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:2537). I reproduced that targeted test locally; it reported `label-uniqueness over 400 issues took 660.2µs`.

Scope semantics are documented in [docs/reference/configuration.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/reference/configuration.md:139).

## Architecture And Separation Of Concerns

Pass. Rule parsing stays in `validation/rules.rs`, pure graph evaluation stays in `validation/graph.rs`, and docs/examples remain outside runtime boundaries. No storage, command, CLI, or output layering violations found.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. The evaluator uses a small imperative aggregation loop in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:409), which is appropriate here for a single-pass `HashMap` accumulation and avoids accidental quadratic behavior.

## Correctness And Invariants

Pass. The implementation is read-only validation logic: no file writes, event logging, gate transition mutation, assignee/label mutation, PID handling, or unsafe code are introduced. Repo-wide transition semantics are explicitly guarded by `is_repo_wide_at_transition` and tested via [crates/jit/tests/example_rulesets_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/example_rulesets_tests.rs:963).

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed surface.

## Testing

Pass. Provided run history shows `cargo-ci` passed. I also ran:

- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`: passed, with allowed label warnings only.
- `cargo test -p jit test_label_uniqueness_large_fixture_correctness -- --nocapture`: passed, measured 400-issue evaluation at `660.2µs`.

The prior review’s performance-test gap is fixed by the timed assertion in [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:2552).

## Error Handling

Pass. Invalid `label-uniqueness` scopes return contextual `RuleConfigError::InvalidAssertion` errors in [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1422). No new production `unwrap()` or `expect()` concerns found.

## CLI Contract

Pass. No new CLI command is added. Existing validation behavior remains available through the validation command surface, including JSON-capable CLI paths outside this change.

## Documentation

Pass. The public rule semantics, transition-time limitation, and supported scope values are documented in [docs/reference/configuration.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/reference/configuration.md:145) and the cross-epic example explains the intended collision use case in [docs/examples/cross-epic/rules.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/examples/cross-epic/rules.toml:6).

## Prior Review Feedback

Pass. The most recent failed review identified only the missing measured performance evidence. The follow-up commit adds actual elapsed-time measurement and an anti-regression assertion for the large fixture.

## JIT Issue Management

Pass. The issue has no dependencies. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passes; label warnings are allowed by the review instructions.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
